info.name: Spin Platform API
info.description:
Spin Platform API — NestJS + MySQL. **Setup:** `baseUrl` (default `http://localhost:3000`), then **Auth → Login** → `accessToken`.

**Envelope:** `{ error, message, statusCode, responseData }` (see **Health** for public shape). **Exception:** **Public — QR resolve** returns **302** with `Location` (not the usual JSON envelope).

**API index (example bodies live in each request’s Body / URL):**
- **Health:** `GET /`
- **Auth:** `POST /auth/register`, `POST /auth/login`
- **RBAC:** `GET /rbac/me`, `GET/POST /rbac/permissions`, `DELETE /rbac/permissions/:id`, `GET/POST/PATCH /rbac/roles`, `POST /rbac/roles/:roleId/permissions`, `POST/DELETE /rbac/users/:userId/roles`
- **Campaigns:** `GET /campaigns` (optional `?page&limit&search&status`), `POST /campaigns`, `PATCH /campaigns/:id`, `POST /campaigns/:id/products`
- **Products:** `GET /products` (`?search=`), `POST /products`, `GET/PATCH/DELETE /products/:id`
- **Admin — Prize config:** `GET/POST /admin/prizes`, `GET /admin/prize-config/:campaignId`, `POST /admin/prize-config`
- **Admin — Probability:** `GET /admin/probability-config/:campaignId`, `POST /admin/probability-config`
- **Admin — QR frontend settings:** `GET/PUT /admin/qr-frontend-settings` — persisted **`frontendBaseUrl`** (not env); `PUT` refreshes every mapping’s **`redirectUrl`**
- **Admin — QR mappings:** `GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /admin/qr-mappings` — `redirectUrl` uses saved frontend base + `/campaign?qr={code}` (fallback `http://localhost:3000` when unset)
- **Campaign landing (public):** `GET /campaign?qr=:code`
- **Lead submission (public):** `GET /leads/gifts?qrCode=:code`, `POST /leads/submit` (multipart)
- **Leads (auth):** `GET /leads` (`?page&limit&search&campaignId`)
- **Receipt (auth):** `POST /receipt/upload` (multipart)
- **Spin (public):** `POST /spin`
- **Public — QR resolve:** `GET /qr/:code` → **302** to stored `redirectUrl` (throttled)

**Variables:** `accessToken`, `campaignId`, `productId`, `productId1`, `productId2`, `prizeId`, `prizeId2`, `roleId`, `targetUserId`, `permissionId`, `qrMappingId`, `qrCode`, `participationId`.

variable.description: Numeric user id from POST /auth/register or seed (admin = 1)
Numeric permission id from POST /rbac/permissions or GET /rbac/permissions
Numeric campaign id from POST /campaigns (test script) or GET /campaigns
Product id for GET/PATCH/DELETE — set by Create product or List products script
First product id — updated by List products / Create product scripts
Second product id — from List products script
First prize id — Create prize (Gold) or List prizes script
Second prize id — Create prize (Silver) or List prizes script
From List QR mappings or Create QR mapping
Numeric participation id from POST /leads/submit responseData.participationId

request.description: Public. Example: `{ error: false, message: "Spin Platform API is running", statusCode: 200, responseData: { status: "ok" } }`.

exec: console.log('Set targetUserId to new user:', rd.id);

request.description: Public. Body: `email`, `password` (min 8). Response: `responseData: { id, email }`, `message`: e.g. Registration successful. Test script reads `responseData.id` → `targetUserId`.
Alternate example payload. Change `email` if already registered (`409`). Overwrites `targetUserId` on success.
Public. Example body: `email`, `password`. Response: `{ error, message, statusCode, responseData: { accessToken, admin } }`. Test script saves `responseData.accessToken` to `accessToken`.

item.name: RBAC
item.description: Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}`. Admin routes need `rbac:manage` (seeded **admin** user).

request.description: JWT only. `responseData`: `{ user: { id, email }, permissions: string[] }` (RBAC routes use default `message: Success`).
Requires `rbac:manage`.

Requires `rbac:manage`. Body: `permissionKey` (`resource:action`, stored lowercased), optional `description`. Change `permissionKey` if you already created this one (409 duplicate). Test script saves returned `id` to `permissionId`.
Requires `rbac:manage`. Set `permissionId` (e.g. run **Create permission** first, or copy id from **List permissions**). Removes the row and cascades `role_permissions` links.

exec: console.log('Set roleId to admin role:', admin.id);

request.description: Requires `rbac:manage`. Test script sets `roleId` to the **admin** role id when present.
Requires `rbac:manage`. `description` is optional.
Requires `rbac:manage`. Set `roleId` (e.g. run **List roles** first). Send only fields to change.
Requires `rbac:manage`. Replaces all permissions for the role with this list. Keys must exist in DB (see seed / permission-keys).
Requires `rbac:manage`. Path `userId` = user id. Body `roleId` = role id (often same as collection `roleId`). Set `targetUserId` from Register response.
Requires `rbac:manage`. Removes the role from the user.

item.name: Campaigns
item.description: Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}`. Same envelope as other APIs: `error`, `message`, `statusCode`, `responseData`.

request.description: Returns all campaigns with nested `products` (id, name). No query params.
Optional query: `page`, `limit` (1–100), `search` (name substring), `status` = `ACTIVE` | `INACTIVE`. Omit both `page` and `limit` to list all campaigns without pagination metadata.
`name` must be unique; `endDate` must be after `startDate`; `status` is `ACTIVE` or `INACTIVE`. Test script saves `responseData.id` → `campaignId`.
Alternate example payload (different `name`/dates). Overwrites `campaignId` on success. Use if **Summer Spin 2026** already exists.

Full example body (all fields). Partial updates allowed — send only fields to change. Set `campaignId` (e.g. run **Create campaign** first). Validates date range when both `startDate` and `endDate` are evaluated against each other.
Example minimal PATCH: only `status` (`ACTIVE` | `INACTIVE`). Omit dates to avoid date-range validation on update.

`replaceExisting: true` removes existing mappings for this campaign, then adds the listed products. All `productIds` must exist. Replace `[1,2,3]` with real ids from **Products → List products**. Set `campaignId` first.

item.name: Map products to campaign (collection product ids)

request.description: Uses `productId1` and `productId2` from collection variables (defaults 1,2 — run **List products** to refresh from DB). `replaceExisting: false` keeps existing mappings and adds new ones (duplicates ignored).

item.name: Products
item.description: Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}`. Unified envelope; **Create** = `201`. **Delete** uses `responseData: null` and top-level `message`.

exec: console.log('Set productId / productId1 / productId2 from list');

request.description: `responseData` is an array of `{ id, name }`. Test script sets `productId`, `productId1`, `productId2`.
Optional query `search` — substring match on product `name` (wildcards stripped server-side).
`name` required, trimmed, unique (`409` if duplicate). `responseData`: `{ id, name }`. Change `name` if duplicate.
Alternate example body `{ "name": "Cold Drink" }`. Change name if duplicate. Run **List products** to refresh `productId1` / `productId2`.
Returns `data`: `{ id, name }`. `404` if not found. Set `productId` via **List products** or **Create product**.
Body must include `name` (trimmed, unique). `404` not found; `409` duplicate name.
Safe delete: `409` if linked to a campaign. Success: `responseData: null`, `message`: Product deleted successfully.

item.name: Admin — Prize config
item.description: JWT required. Configure **prizes**, per-**campaign** product→prize **mappings**, **inventory**, and **distribution limits**. Run **Campaigns → Create campaign**, **Products** (and map products to campaign), **Create prize** / **List prizes** so `campaignId`, `productId1`, `prizeId` are valid before **Save prize config**.

request.description: `responseData`: `[{ id, name }, ...]`. Test script sets `prizeId` / `prizeId2` from first two rows (for **Probability** multi-prize examples).
`201`. Example body: `{ "name": "Gold Spin Voucher" }`. Test script saves `responseData.id` → `prizeId`.
Second example prize; change `name` if duplicate. Saves `responseData.id` → `prizeId2`. Run after **Create prize** (Gold) or use a unique name.

Example `responseData`: `{ campaignId, mappings: [{productId, prizeId}], inventory: [{prizeId, stock}], distributionLimits: { maxPerDay, maxPerUser, totalLimit } }`. Empty arrays / zero limits if never saved. `404` if campaign missing.

item.name: Save prize config (example payload — static ids)

request.description: Full replace in one **transaction**. Edit ids: `productId` must be on `campaign_products` for `campaignId`; `prizeId` must exist in `prizes`; inventory prize ids must **match** mapping prize ids exactly; `maxPerUser` and `maxPerDay` ≤ `totalLimit`. `409` on unique violations.
Same contract as static example; uses `{{campaignId}}`, `{{productId1}}`, `{{prizeId}}`. Ensure campaign has product **mapped** via **Campaigns → Map products** before saving.
Clears all mappings and inventory for the campaign; still upserts `prize_configs` (distribution limits only).

item.name: Admin — Probability
item.description: Weighted spin probabilities per **campaign**. Weights must **sum to 100**, exactly **one** `prizeId: null` (lose), at least one **winning** prize with weight > 0, integers only. `POST` replaces all rows in a **transaction**. Import **Prize** ids from **Admin — Prize config**.

request.description: Example `responseData`: `{ campaignId, probabilities: [{ prizeId: 1, weight: 5 }, { prizeId: null, weight: 85 }] }`. `probabilities` is `[]` until first **Save probability config**. `404` if campaign missing.
Replace all probability rows for `campaignId`. Use real `prizeId` values from your DB. JSON `null` for lose row. Sum weights = 100.
Two-outcome example: one prize + lose = 100. Set `campaignId` and `prizeId` (e.g. **Create prize**). For multiple prizes add rows; keep exactly one `prizeId: null`.

item.name: Save probability config (two prizes + lose — variables)

request.description: Weights 10+5+85=100. Requires two distinct prizes: run **List prizes** or **Create prize** + **Create prize (Silver)**. `prizeId` ≠ `prizeId2` or duplicate validation fails.

item.name: Admin — QR mappings
item.description: CRUD for `qr_mappings`. **Create** accepts optional `code` (URL-safe alnum + `_-`); if omitted, server generates a random code. **`redirectUrl`** uses the saved **QR frontend base** (`GET/PUT /admin/qr-frontend-settings`), or `http://localhost:3000` until you configure it. **Delete** is a soft delete (`isActive: false`). **Patch** is rejected (`403`) when inactive or expired. Campaign must exist and be **ACTIVE**.

request.description: `responseData`: `{ frontendBaseUrl, effectiveFrontendBaseUrl }`. Saved value is `null` until **Save QR frontend base URL**; effective base defaults to `http://localhost:3000`.

Body **must** include `frontendBaseUrl` (http/https origin, no path) or **`null`** to clear. Persists to DB and **recomputes `redirectUrl` on every QR mapping**; `responseData.qrMappingsUpdated` is the row count.

exec: console.log('Set qrMappingId / qrCode from first row');

request.description: `responseData`: array of `{ id, code, campaignId, campaignName, redirectUrl, isActive, expiresAt, createdAt }`.
Omit `code` → server generates unique URL-safe code. Optional `expiresAt` ISO string.
`409` if `code` duplicate. Pattern: `^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$`.
Optional: `campaignId`, `expiresAt` (null clears), `isActive`. `403` if row inactive/expired before update.
Sets `isActive: false`. `responseData`: `null`.

item.name: Campaign landing (public)
item.description: Resolve landing page campaign details from QR. QR must exist, be active, not expired, and mapped campaign must be active and within campaign date range.

request.description: Public route used by landing page. Returns `{ campaignId, campaignName, branding, offer }` in `responseData`.

item.name: Lead submission
item.description: Public entry form. Submit lead details + receipt file in **multipart/form-data**. Required fields: `name`, `phone` (7-14 digits), `email`, `address`, `shopLocation`, `qrCode`, `receiptNumber`, `acceptTerms`, and `file` (jpeg/png/webp/pdf, max 5MB). `acceptTerms` must be `true`; `false` is rejected (`400`). Duplicate `receiptNumber` returns `409`.

item.name: Get gift items by QR campaign code

request.description: Public route. Returns all gift/prize items for the campaign mapped to this QR code, with current stock and `inStock` flag.

Public route. `acceptTerms` accepts boolean-like values (`true`/`false`, `1`/`0`). Successful response includes `{ leadId, participationId, receiptId, fileUrl }` in `responseData`. Test script saves `participationId` for `/spin`.
Authenticated route. Pagination params: `page` (>=1), `limit` (1-100), optional `search` (name/email/phone), optional `campaignId`. Returns `responseData.items` + `responseData.pagination`.
Validation example: service rejects `acceptTerms=false` with `400` (`You must accept terms and conditions to continue`).

item.name: Receipt
item.description: Authenticated receipt upload with duplicate detection by SHA-256 hash (and optional pHash similarity).

request.description: Example multipart payload for duplicate-check flow. Requires Bearer token. `email` should match authenticated user email.

item.name: Spin
item.description: Public endpoint. This route uses `@SkipStandardResponse()` and returns raw spin payload on success. Use `participationId` from **Lead submission → Submit lead with receipt**.

request.description: Example body requires `participationId` (integer >= 1). Run **Lead submission → Submit lead with receipt** first to auto-populate `{{participationId}}`.
Static payload example for quick smoke testing when variable values are not configured.

item.name: Public — QR resolve
item.description: **No Bearer token.** Returns **302 Found** with `Location` = stored `redirectUrl`. Postman may auto-follow redirects (disable in request settings to see **302** + headers). Rate-limited (throttler).

request.description: Run **List** or **Create** in **Admin — QR mappings** first so `{{qrCode}}` is set. Inactive/expired → `403` JSON envelope via exception filter.
